title: thevetia cuneifolia
intro: Thevetia cuneifolia, also known as the yellow oleander or yellow cascade, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family. It is known for its toxic properties, particularly the presence of thevetin A and thevetin B, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Thevetia cuneifolia have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Thevetia cuneifolia are often made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution should be exercised due to its toxic nature, as excessive consumption can be harmful.
